1: O LORD LORD my God, thou art very great; Thou art clothed with honour and majesty:
2: Who stretchest out the heavens like a curtain:
3: Who maketh the clouds his chariot: Who walketh upon the wings of the wind:
5: That That it should not be removed for ever.
6: The waters stood above the mountains.
7: At the voice of thy thunder they hasted away.
8: Unto the place which thou hast founded for them.
9: That they turn not again to cover the earth.
10: Which Which run among the hills.
11: The wild asses quench their thirst.
12: Which Which sing among the branches.
13: The earth is satisfied with the fruit of thy works.
14: That he may bring forth food out of the earth;
15: And And oil to make his his face to shine, and bread which which strengtheneth man's heart.
16: The cedars of Lebanon, which he hath planted;
17: As for As for the stork, the fir trees are are her house.
18: And And the rocks for the conies.
19: The sun knoweth his going down.
20: Wherein all the beasts of the forest do creep forth forth .
21: And seek their meat from God.
22: And lay them down in their dens.
23: And to his labour until the evening.
24: In wisdom hast thou made them all: The earth is full of thy riches.
25: Wherein are are things creeping innumerable, both small and great beasts.
26: Whom Whom thou hast made to play therein.
27: That thou mayest give them them their meat in due season.
28: Thou openest thine hand, they are filled with good.
29: Thou takest away their breath, they die, and return to their dust.
30: And thou renewest the face of the earth.
31: The LORD LORD shall rejoice in his works.
32: He toucheth the hills, and they smoke.
33: I will sing praise to my God while I have my being.
34: I will be glad in the LORD LORD .
